title: Letter to self on a Phoenix→Portland flight, eve of school year
html:
TL;DR

Written on a flight from Phoenix to portland or the night before school starts. Yahir is using the act of writing to silence ("low key writing this to end... more like slightly silence") feelings for alexandria nagy, a girl he just met. He acknowledges she was special, then explicitly orders himself not to let it become an anchor. The note pivots into a self-pep-talk about advancing, accomplishing, and proving people wrong.

Key quotes

"I am low key writing this to end, more like slightly silence my thoughts or feeling for the girl you meet, Alexandria Nagy."

"Remember Alexandria, because she was special, but don't get trapped or glued or even stuck on her, advance in this life and world."

"Let's have a good time and a great time. That is what life is all about."

Why it matters in the graph

The earliest clean specimen of the note as letter to self form Yahir uses for emotional processing — second-person, present-tense, with an explicit "kill the feeling and march" ending. The same template recurs across his late-night entries through 2025–2026.

Open questions

  • Did Alexandria recur enough to deserve more than a sparse stub? (mid-2019 has at least 3 filename hits — keep watching)
  • Does Yahir's "advance / don't get trapped" framing show up around later relationships, or did this template change?

Wikilinks
